categories.name: Home
categories.prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency that sells digital solutions to local service businesses.

Analyze this locksmith's homepage for digital maturity and gaps. You are NOT judging quality — you are identifying sales opportunities.

Critical for locksmiths: a locked-out person on their phone needs to reach help in under 30 seconds.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No click-to-call button visible above the fold
- No service area mentioned
- No emergency availability stated (24/7)
- Slow load (critical for panicked mobile users)
- No trust signals (license, insurance, reviews)
- Generic template with stock photos
- No schema markup

COLD LEAD signals:
- Prominent emergency phone number, click-to-call
- Service area clearly listed
- 24/7 availability stated
- Fast mobile load
- Trust badges: license, insurance, trade body membership
- Real photos of vans, team, work
- LocalBusiness schema with service area

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of digital presence>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = more gaps = better lead>",
  "digitalMaturityScore": "<1-10, higher = more mature>",
  "hasClickToCall": true/false,
  "hasServiceArea": true/false,
  "hasEmergencyInfo": true/false,
  "hasTrustSignals": true/false,
  "hasMobileExperience": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<single biggest gap a web agency could sell>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

categories.name: Services
categories.extraInfo: services, lockout, lock change, lock repair, emergency, commercial, residential, auto
categories.prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's services page.

HOT LEAD signals:
- All services dumped on one page with no structure
- No individual service pages (bad for SEO)
- Missing service descriptions — just a bullet list
- No distinction between emergency and scheduled work
- No photos of actual work

COLD LEAD signals:
- Individual pages per service type (residential, commercial, auto, emergency)
- Detailed descriptions of what each service involves
- Clear emergency vs scheduled distinction
- Service-specific CTAs
- Before/after photos
- FAQ per service

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of services presentation>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = worse services page = better lead>",
  "serviceStructure": "<individual-pages|single-page|minimal|none>",
  "servicesListed": ["<service types found>"],
  "hasEmergencyDistinction": true/false,
  "hasServiceDescriptions": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

categories.name: Pricing
categories.extraInfo: pricing, prices, costs, rates, quotes, call out charge, estimate
categories.prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's pricing transparency.

"Call for quote" is the norm in this industry — any pricing transparency is a competitive advantage.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No pricing information at all
- "Call for a quote" with no guidance
- No mention of callout charges
- No indication of price range

COLD LEAD signals:
- Starting-from prices for common jobs
- Callout charge clearly stated
- Price calculator or estimator
- "No hidden fees" guarantee
- Price comparison vs competitors
- Free quote form with structured inputs

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of pricing transparency>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = less transparent = better lead>",
  "pricingType": "<listed|ranges|call-only|none>",
  "hasCalloutCharge": true/false,
  "hasQuoteForm": true/false,
  "hasPriceGuarantee": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

categories.name: Trust
categories.extraInfo: about, reviews, testimonials, accreditation, license, insurance, guarantee, trade body, MLA
categories.prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's trust signals.

Locksmiths have a massive trust problem — the industry is plagued by scam operators. Trust signals are critical for conversion.

HOT LEAD signals:
- No reviews or testimonials on site
- No mention of license or insurance
- No trade body membership (MLA, ALOA)
- No guarantee mentioned
- Stock photos of locks instead of real team
- No Google Reviews integration

COLD LEAD signals:
- Embedded Google/Trustpilot reviews
- Trade body logos (Master Locksmiths Association)
- License and insurance numbers
- Named team members with photos
- Guarantee/warranty stated
- Case studies or before/after photos
- Years in business prominently displayed

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of trust signals>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = fewer trust signals = better lead>",
  "hasReviews": true/false,
  "hasTradeBody": true/false,
  "hasInsurance": true/false,
  "hasNamedTeam": true/false,
  "hasGuarantee": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

categories.name: Contact
categories.extraInfo: contact, emergency, call us, find us, service area, coverage
categories.prompt:
You are a B2B prospecting analyst for a web agency. Analyze this locksmith's contact/emergency page.

Critical question: can a locked-out person get help within 30 seconds of landing on this page?

HOT LEAD signals:
- Phone number not prominent or not click-to-call
- No service area map or list
- Contact form as primary contact method (useless in emergency)
- No response time stated
- No after-hours information

COLD LEAD signals:
- Giant click-to-call button
- Service area map with coverage zones
- Response time guarantee ("30 min or less")
- 24/7 clearly stated with emergency number
- Live chat
- WhatsApp/text option
- Address with embedded map

Return each record as:
{
  "url": "<page url>",
  "pageName": "<short name>",
  "summary": "<1-2 sentence overview of contact/emergency access>",
  "opportunityScore": "<1-10, higher = harder to reach = better lead>",
  "hasClickToCall": true/false,
  "hasServiceAreaMap": true/false,
  "hasResponseTime": true/false,
  "has24x7Info": true/false,
  "hasLiveChat": true/false,
  "topOpportunity": "<what a web agency could sell here>",
  "notes": "<one sentence prospecting assessment>"
}

records.summary: Banham is a premium, heritage-focused London security brand with a polished editorial homepage emphasising craftsmanship and brand storytelling. The site reads more like a luxury lifestyle destination than an emergency locksmith, with no visible click-to-call, no 24/7 emergency messaging, and no service area callout above the fold.
records.topOpportunity: Add a prominent emergency click-to-call button and 24/7 availability statement above the fold to capture high-intent locked-out mobile users who will otherwise bounce instantly
records.notes: Banham is a mature, well-branded business that has prioritised brand prestige over conversion urgency — a mid-tier opportunity for an agency to pitch emergency UX optimisation and local SEO schema rather than a full rebuild.

records.summary: A clean, consultative landing page for Banham's free in-person security survey, well structured with a clear step-by-step process and FAQ section. However, it relies entirely on a form enquiry flow with no phone number, no click-to-call, and no urgency signals for users with immediate needs.
records.topOpportunity: Introduce a visible phone number or click-to-call CTA alongside the enquiry form to reduce friction for users who want immediate human contact rather than a callback workflow
records.notes: The page is well-designed for considered buyers but lacks any fast-contact pathway, making it a weak conversion point for higher-urgency prospects — a targeted CRO pitch around contact accessibility would resonate here.

records.summary: Page content was not provided, so structure and detail level cannot be assessed. Based on URL alone, this appears to be a single locksmith service landing page.
records.topOpportunity: Unable to assess without page content — potential for dedicated emergency locksmith page, service breakdowns, and local SEO structure
records.notes: No page content available to analyze; manual review required before qualifying as hot or cold lead.

records.summary: A highly polished product-focused page showcasing Banham's lock range (nightlatches, mortice, sash, lock sets) with detailed descriptions, FAQs, and brand heritage storytelling. This is a retail/product page, not a locksmith services page, and is extremely well-structured.
records.topOpportunity: No meaningful web agency opportunity — this is a sophisticated, well-resourced brand with mature digital presence
records.notes: Banham is a premium 100-year-old British security brand with professional web infrastructure; not a viable SME prospecting target.

records.summary: Page content was not provided for analysis. URL suggests a dedicated burglar alarms category page, which would be a cold lead signal if properly structured.
records.topOpportunity: No identifiable opportunity without content — Banham's overall site sophistication suggests minimal gap
records.notes: Banham's brand scale and existing digital investment make this an unlikely prospect for a web agency pitch.

records.summary: Page content was not provided for analysis. URL suggests a dedicated CCTV and entry systems page, consistent with Banham's structured, service-segmented site architecture.
records.topOpportunity: No clear opportunity identifiable — dedicated page structure already in place
records.notes: Consistent with a mature enterprise-level site; not a viable cold outreach target for a web agency.

records.summary: Page content was not provided for analysis. URL indicates a standalone safes category page, further confirming Banham's well-segmented site structure.
records.topOpportunity: No identifiable gap without content review — existing individual-page structure already a cold lead indicator
records.notes: Banham's multi-page, category-segmented architecture across all service types signals a cold lead across the entire domain.

records.summary: Banham provides a dedicated pricing page for their keyholding service, listing clear annual subscription tiers with specific pound amounts for different service levels. This is unusually transparent for the industry, with named packages and stated prices visible without needing to call.
records.topOpportunity: Very limited — pricing is already well-presented; could potentially pitch UX improvements or a price comparison tool, but this is a strong lead for transparency not conversion issues.
records.notes: Banham is a cold lead — their pricing page is well-structured and transparent, making them one of the least promising prospects for a web agency pitch around pricing visibility.

records.pageName: Great British Brands Product of the Year 2026
records.summary: Banham showcases a prestigious 2026 product award, a century of family business heritage, and named co-managing directors — all strong trust signals. The page emphasises craftsmanship, British manufacturing, and a robust product range with specialist team support in showrooms.
records.topOpportunity: Adding embedded Google/Trustpilot reviews and a trade body membership badge section could round out an already strong trust page
records.notes: Banham is a well-established, highly credible brand with strong heritage and award proof points — very cold lead with minimal web agency opportunity.

records.summary: This page details Banham's patented lock and key systems, including the Mark III patent valid until 2032, emphasising legal exclusivity, key registration, and a decades-long history of innovation. It functions as a strong technical trust signal demonstrating proprietary security credentials.
records.topOpportunity: Integrating customer testimonials or case studies to complement the technical patent content with social proof
records.notes: Banham's patent page is a sophisticated trust asset that few competitors can match — this is a cold lead representing a mature, self-sufficient brand.

records.summary: The page offers multiple contact routes including phone numbers for existing customers, new enquiries, and a dedicated 24/7 emergency locksmith line (020 7622 5151), but buries the emergency number mid-page among many other query categories. The primary layout pushes users toward lengthy dropdown-driven contact forms rather than surfacing the emergency number immediately at the top.
records.topOpportunity: Redesign the emergency contact UX: a sticky top-of-page click-to-call banner for the 24/7 locksmith line, plus a clear service area map and stated response time guarantee to convert locked-out visitors before they bounce.
records.notes: Banham has the right ingredients (24/7 line, multiple numbers) but hides the emergency CTA deep in a cluttered form-heavy layout — a focused emergency landing page with a prominent click-to-call and response time promise would meaningfully lift conversion.

records.summary: The page lists four London/South England showrooms with addresses and click-to-call phone numbers, an interactive Google Map with pin markers, and a postcode checker to confirm local service availability. However, it is focused on in-person consultations rather than emergency locksmith response, with no 24/7 or response-time information present.
records.topOpportunity: Add an emergency callout CTA section above the fold — a prominent 24/7 locksmith phone button with response time promise — to capture locked-out visitors who land on the showrooms page outside business hours.
records.notes: Strong location and map presence makes this a good prospect for adding an emergency overlay or sticky banner; the postcode checker is a solid foundation but the page completely ignores after-hours emergency needs.

You classify links from a website homepage into buckets. The buckets are:
- "Home": 
- "Services": services, lockout, lock change, lock repair, emergency, commercial, residential, auto
- "Pricing": pricing, prices, costs, rates, quotes, call out charge, estimate
- "Trust": about, reviews, testimonials, accreditation, license, insurance, guarantee, trade body, MLA
- "Contact": contact, emergency, call us, find us, service area, coverage
- "other": everything else

Return ONLY a JSON object with one key per bucket name. Each value is an array of
matched URLs (use the exact href from the input), except "other" which you may omit.
Cap each named bucket at 5 URLs. Prefer the most informative URL
when forced to choose. Use labels as the primary signal, URLs as fallback.
